evals.prompt: I want to set up Allure reporting for my project — I've never used it before. My vitest config is at vitest.config.ts and I already have tests running with vitest
evals.expected_output: allure-vitest is installed, vitest.config.ts is updated with the Allure reporter alongside the default reporter, .gitignore is updated, and an HTML report is generated and served.
assertions: The skill checks package.json for existing allure-vitest before installing
The skill checks vitest.config.ts for an existing Allure reporter entry
npm install -D allure-vitest allure-js-commons is executed
vitest.config.ts is updated to include 'allure-vitest/reporter' in the reporters array
The 'default' reporter is preserved in the reporters array
The resultsDir is set to './allure-results'
allure-results/ and allure-report/ are added to .gitignore
npm test -- --run is executed to produce test results
npx allure generate is executed with --clean flag
The report path ./allure-report/index.html is communicated to the user
The reopen command npx allure open ./allure-report is communicated to the user

evals.prompt: I just ran my BDD tests and they all pass. Can you generate an Allure report? I already have allure-vitest configured
evals.expected_output: The skill detects that Allure is already configured, skips setup, and generates the HTML report directly from existing allure-results/.
assertions: The skill checks package.json and confirms allure-vitest is present
The skill checks vitest.config.ts and confirms the reporter is configured
The skill does NOT run npm install for allure-vitest (already installed)
The skill does NOT modify vitest.config.ts (already configured)
npx allure generate ./allure-results --clean -o ./allure-report is executed
The report path and reopen command are communicated to the user

evals.prompt: set up Allure for my project. I already have a custom reporter configured in vitest.config.ts — make sure you don't remove it. My config has reporters: ['default', 'junit']
evals.expected_output: allure-vitest is installed and added to the reporters array alongside both 'default' and 'junit' — neither existing reporter is removed.
assertions: The skill reads vitest.config.ts before modifying it
The 'default' reporter is preserved in the reporters array
The 'junit' reporter is preserved in the reporters array
The 'allure-vitest/reporter' entry is added to the reporters array
The resulting reporters array contains all three reporters
npm install -D allure-vitest allure-js-commons is executed
allure-results/ and allure-report/ are added to .gitignore if not already present
npx allure generate is executed after tests run

evals.prompt: generate an Allure report — Allure is configured but the allure-results/ directory is empty
evals.expected_output: The skill detects that allure-results/ is empty and informs the user they need to run tests first to produce results.
assertions: The skill checks if allure-results/ directory exists
The skill detects the directory is empty (no .json result files)
The skill informs the user that tests need to be run first to produce Allure results
The skill offers to run npm test -- --run to produce results
The skill does NOT run npx allure generate on an empty results directory
